name: Moffat County Schools
description_short: Moffat County Schools Federal Credit Union is a small, well-capitalized credit union chartered in 1962, serving educational employees and their families in Craig, Colorado with basic financial services.
description_long:
Moffat County Schools Federal Credit Union was established in 1962 and operates as a multiple common bond credit union primarily serving educational professionals and their families. The credit union is headquartered in Craig, Colorado, and maintains a single branch location at 585 Tucker Street. As of Q1 2024, the institution serves 849 members with a modest but stable financial foundation. The credit union is regulated by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA) and holds charter number 15230.

The credit union offers traditional banking services including savings and deposit accounts, as well as lending products such as auto loans and mortgages. Members can access financial management tools and historical trend data for auto loans and mortgage products. The institution maintains a conservative operational structure with 2 full-time employees and no part-time staff, keeping costs low. The credit union does not operate ATMs, requiring members to rely on branch visits or external networks for cash access.

Moffat County Schools FCU distinguishes itself through exceptional financial strength. As of Q1 2024, the credit union maintains a net worth ratio of 13.23, placing it firmly in the "well-capitalized" category (7.0 or higher is required). Total assets stand at $5.7 million, with total loans of $2.9 million and strong liquidity of $2.7 million in cash and deposits. The credit union generated $15,434 in net income during Q1 2024 with a healthy net interest margin of $55,742, demonstrating operational efficiency despite its small size.

This institution is best suited for educational professionals with existing connections to Moffat County schools who value personal service and financial stability over extensive product offerings or digital convenience. The small member base, minimal staffing, and lack of ATMs indicate a deliberately lean operation rather than a full-service financial institution. Potential members should verify current service offerings and product availability directly with the credit union, as the publicly available data does not comprehensively detail all services, fee structures, or lending requirements. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Auto loan origination and portfolio management
Mortgage lending for home purchase and refinancing
Loan allowance and credit loss management
Member financial reporting and performance data access
Auto loan trend analysis and historical data
Mortgage trend reporting and historical loan data
Fee-based income services (overdraft, ATM, credit card fees)
Basic cash and deposit management services
pros: Well-capitalized with net worth ratio of 13.23, indicating strong financial stability and low risk
Long operating history since 1962 provides institutional experience and member trust
Low operating costs reflected in 2 full-time employees and efficient operations
Strong liquidity position with $2.7M in cash and deposits against $5.7M total assets
Member-focused field of membership serving educational professionals and their families
NCUA-insured deposits provide federal insurance protection up to $250,000 per account
cons: No ATM access on premises—members must travel to branch or use external networks
Very small member base (849 members) limits resources and service expansion potential
Single branch location in Craig, CO creates geographic limitation for member access
Limited product information publicly available; auto loan and mortgage details not fully specified
Minimal staffing (2 full-time employees) may restrict service hours and availability
best_for: Educational professionals employed by Moffat County schools seeking relationship banking
Local Craig, Colorado residents with school system connections preferring credit union membership
Members prioritizing financial institution stability and well-capitalized status over convenience
Individuals seeking membership in a small, established, not-for-profit financial cooperative
diagnosis: Moffat County Schools Federal Credit Union is best for educational professionals already connected to Moffat County schools who value strong capitalization, member-focused service, and not-for-profit membership over product variety or convenience. The main caveat is that this institution's very small size (849 members, 2 employees, single branch) and lack of ATM access make it impractical for members seeking digital banking, extended service hours, or robust product selection—contact them directly to confirm current offerings before applying.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
